title: Some Basic Concept of Chemistry Test - 2 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: Two compounds I and II made up of carbon and oxygen contain 72.73% and 47.06% oxygen respectively. Therefore, they obey
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which is not a basic postulate of Dalton's atomic theory?
options: Atoms are neither created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction.
Different elements have different types of atoms.
Atoms of an element may be different due to presence of isotopes.
Each element is composed of extremely small particles called atoms.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: 12 g of alkaline earth metal gives 14.8 g of its nitride. Atomic weight of metal is -
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The law of multiple proportion is illustrated by the two compounds:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If samples of water are collected from the sea, rivers, or lakes, each sample is found to have hydrogen and oxygen present in nearly the same mass ratio of 1 : 8. This observation supports which law?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Hydrogen and oxygen react to give H2O2 and H2O. In these compounds, the percentage of hydrogen is 5.93% and 11.2% respectively. This information shows the :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Carbon reacts with oxygen to give two compounds, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. In these, the mass ratio of carbon to oxygen is 12 : 16 and 12 : 32 respectively. These data show the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A graph is plotted for an element by putting its mass on X-axis and the corresponding number of atoms on Y-axis. What is the atomic mass of the element for which the graph is plotted?(NA = 6.0 × 1023)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Consider a molecule of H2O composed of H1 and O16 isotopes. If the mass of a neutron is increased to twice its value and the mass of a proton is reduced by half, the relative molecular mass of water will
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-1 : Gram molecular weight of O2 is 32 g.

Statement-2 : Relative atomic weight of oxygen is 32.

Choose the correct option :
options: Statement-1 is true, statement-2 is true and statement-2 is correct explanation for statement-1.
Statement-1 is true, statement-2 is true and statement-2 is not correct explanation for statement-1.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Zinc ore (zinc sulphide) is reacted with sulphuric acid. After the reaction, a mixture of solution and some unreacted solid remains, and hydrogen sulphide gas is produced. When 10.8 g of zinc ore is treated with 50.0 ml of sulphuric acid (density $1.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{ml}$), the mass of the remaining solution along with the undissolved solid is 65.2 g. Hydrogen sulphide gas (density $1.4 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{L}$) is also evolved. Find the volume (in litres) of this gas.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An element X exists in three isotopic forms: $\mathrm{X}^{20}$, $\mathrm{X}^{21}$, and $\mathrm{X}^{22}$. The isotope $\mathrm{X}^{20}$ is present at $90\%$ abundance, and the element has an average atomic mass of 20.18. What is the percentage abundance of $\mathrm{X}^{21}$?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A sample contains $2 \times 10^{21}$ molecules of P mixed with $3 \times 10^{21}$ molecules of Q. The total mass of this mixture is 0.60 g. If the molecular mass of P is 45, then the molecular mass of Q is $\left(N_{\mathrm{A}}=6 \times 10^{23}\right)$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A mixture is made up of only two pure substances, X and Y. The total mass of this mixture is 3.72 g and the total amount of substance is 0.06 mol. The molar mass of Y is 48 g mol⁻¹, and the mixture contains 0.02 mol of X. Find the molar mass of X.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: While determining atomic masses, Dalton assumed the formula of water to be HO. He also took the composition of water as hydrogen $=12.5 \%$ and oxygen $=87.5 \%$ by mass. Using this information, what will be the atomic mass of oxygen on the H-scale?
questions.subject: Chemistry
